schema.name: TZPID source truth registry gap fill

theory.canonical_statement: The Hamiltonian source provides canonical momentum coordinates, the phase-space Hamiltonian, the Poisson bracket, and Hamiltonian evolution.
theory.technical_interpretation: The equation support layer follows \pi_{\omega}=\frac{\partial\mathcal{L}}{\partial\dot{\omega}_{\mathrm{gyro}}}=I_{\mathrm{gyro}}\omega_{\mathrm{gyro}}+\frac{g_{\mathrm{factor}}\mu_B}{\hbar}\mu_{\mathrm{total}}; \pi_r=\frac{\partial\mathcal{L}}{\partial\dot{r}_{\mathrm{cm}}}=m_{\mathrm{total}}\dot{r}_{\mathrm{cm}}; \mathcal{H}[\Psi,\pi,t]=\pi_{\omega}\cdot\omega_{\mathrm{gyro}}+\pi_r\cdot\dot{r}_{\mathrm{cm}}-\mathcal{L}_{\mathrm{total}}[\Psi,\dot{\Psi},t]; \frac{d\Psi}{dt}=\{\Psi,\mathcal{H}\}_{\mathrm{Poisson}}. These relations are compact review objects, not unsupported placeholders.

reviewed_source_symbols.description: canonical momentum conjugate to gyroscopic angular velocity.
canonical momentum conjugate to center-of-mass position.
total Lagrangian entering the Legendre transform.

dictionary_sections.interpretive_role: This packet converts malformed or empty registry material into a usable production section based on cited D-drive evidence.
dictionary_sections.tzp_thesaurus:
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=1.6em,itemsep=6pt,topsep=4pt]
\item Canonical carrier: the repaired mathematical relation used as the entry spine.
\item Source grounding: the D-drive file or row that supplies the relation.
\item TRAWIN closure: the interpretation through time, rotation, action, wave, integration, and normalization.
\end{enumerate}
dictionary_sections.encyclopedia_mathematica: A suitable visual should place the canonical relation at center, source operators as labeled inputs, and the equation-support progression as the lower derivation path.

derivation_layer.source_latex:
% BEGIN TZP DERIVATION LAYER
\tzpsection{Derivation Layer}
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=1.6em,itemsep=7pt,topsep=4pt]
\item
\[
\pi_{\omega}=\frac{\partial\mathcal{L}}{\partial\dot{\omega}_{\mathrm{gyro}}}=I_{\mathrm{gyro}}\omega_{\mathrm{gyro}}+\frac{g_{\mathrm{factor}}\mu_B}{\hbar}\mu_{\mathrm{total}}
\]
This relation is retained from the source-backed repair chain.
\item
\[
\pi_r=\frac{\partial\mathcal{L}}{\partial\dot{r}_{\mathrm{cm}}}=m_{\mathrm{total}}\dot{r}_{\mathrm{cm}}
\]
This relation is retained from the source-backed repair chain.
\item
\[
\mathcal{H}[\Psi,\pi,t]=\pi_{\omega}\cdot\omega_{\mathrm{gyro}}+\pi_r\cdot\dot{r}_{\mathrm{cm}}-\mathcal{L}_{\mathrm{total}}[\Psi,\dot{\Psi},t]
\]
This relation is retained from the source-backed repair chain.
\item
\[
\frac{d\Psi}{dt}=\{\Psi,\mathcal{H}\}_{\mathrm{Poisson}}
\]
This relation is retained from the source-backed repair chain.
\end{enumerate}
% END TZP DERIVATION LAYER
